summary: Flemish Renaissance painter of peasant genre scenes and landscapes. Known for Landscape with the Fall of Icarus (ploughman ignores drowning Icarus), Hunters in the Snow, The Peasant Wedding, Netherlandish Proverbs, The Tower of Babel, Mad Meg, The Triumph of Death. Father of 'Velvet' and 'Hell' Bruegel.

works.name: Landscape with the Fall of Icarus (c. 1560)
works.description: Ploughman in foreground ignores title figure's legs sticking out of sea; ship sails toward setting sun; shepherd with brown dog stares skyward; fisherman ignores Icarus. Existing version and van Buren Museum version are both considered early copies. Inspired Auden's 'Musee des Beaux Arts' and W.C. Williams's ekphrasis. Andre Breton attached a cutout to 'Dusseldorf was bombed yesterday.' Icarus figures (partridge, shepherd, fisherman) derive from Ovid's Metamorphoses. Part of world landscape (Weltlandschaft) tradition. Wikipedia

images.caption: Landscape with the Fall of Icarus

clues.clue: Ploughman ignores Icarus's legs sticking out of the sea; ship sails toward setting sun; shepherd on cliff stares skyward; fisherman also ignores Icarus; shepherd has a brown dog and leans on staff
examples: In a landscape by this painter, the only visible part of the title figure is a pair of legs sticking out of the sea
a ship sails past the flailing feet of the title drowning youth
A fisherman ignores the title figure in a green tinged sea, who is rendered as only a flailing pair of legs
a shepherd with a brown dog leans on his staff to look upward

clues.clue: Existing version may be a copy; both extant versions (including smaller van Buren Museum version) now considered early copies with differences including a sunset near white mountainous islands
examples: Both extant versions of this painting, including the smaller van Buren Museum version, are now considered to be early copies

examples: William Carlos Williams's ekphrasis on this painting describes 'the whole pageantry / of the year was / awake tingling'

clues.clue: Also inspired Wislawa Szymborska poem about Two Monkeys

clues.clue: Part of the world landscape (Weltlandschaft) tradition pioneered by Joachim Patinir
examples: Pieter Bruegel the Elder used [the world landscape genre] to depict the fall of Icarus

works.name: Hunters in the Snow (1565)
works.description: Three hunters and dogs trudge through wintry landscape; skaters and hockey/ice-stock players on frozen lake in distance; dead fox carried. Tavern sign on left reads 'this is in the stag' with haloed man and deer (St. Hubertus). Black birds perched in receding diagonal of bare trees. Frozen water wheel on mill. Woman pulled on board toward double-arched bridge in lower right. Part of Labors of the Months series. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Three hunters and dogs trudge through winter landscape; skaters on frozen lake in distance; one hunter carries dead fox; tavern sign with St. Hubertus and deer reads 'this is in the stag'; black birds perched in trees in receding diagonal
examples: three men and their dogs return from an unsuccessful trip, and silhouettes can be seen ice skating in the distance
In a painting by this artist, a man carrying a dead fox and his companion walk on a hill in the foreground
A tavern sign on the left side of this work features the scene of St. Hubertus and the deer

clues.clue: Part of Labors of the Months series (six paintings; five extant: The Gloomy Day, Haymaking, The Harvesters, The Return of the Herd); this painting is the first in the series
examples: This artist's paintings Haymaking, The Gloomy Day, and The Return of the Herd are among the five extant works in his series of six landscapes
This painting is the first in a series which also includes paintings like The Gloomy Day and The Return of the Herd

clues.clue: Villagers playing ice-stock and hockey visible in frozen lake background; mill with frozen water wheel; woman pulled on board toward double-arched bridge in lower right
examples: He depicted villagers in the distance playing ice-stock and hockey in his landscape Hunters in the Snow
The bottom right shows a woman pulled along on a board toward a double-arched bridge

clues.clue: Used by Abbas Kiarostami in first shot of posthumous film 24 Frames (birds and smoke begin to move)
examples: Birds and smoke from a painting by this artist begin to move in the first shot of Abbas Kiarostami's posthumous film 24 Frames

works.name: The Peasant Wedding
works.description: Celebratory feast in barn; bowls of soup/porridge on makeshift tray made from a door; boy in red cap licks plate; paper crown on green cloth behind bride. Man with spoon in hat. Two wheat sheaves held together by a rake above a man with a spoon in hat (The Peasant Dance companion). Peasant Dance: devotional image of Mary on tree, ignored; man with peacock feather in hair looks at bagpipe player.

clues.clue: Feast in barn; two men carry soup/porridge on a door used as tray; boy in red cap licks plate; paper crown on green cloth; paper-crowned bride not feasting
examples: Bowls of soup and porridge rest on a makeshift tray made of a door
a boy with a red cap covering his eyes licks a plate
showed a paper-crowned bride not feasting in another painting of them

clues.clue: Companion piece The Peasant Dance: devotional image of Mary hanging on a tree on the right, ignored by dancers; man with peacock feather in hair watches bagpipe player
examples: None of the title group of these people pay attention to a devotional image of Mary hanging on a tree on the right-hand side of a painting depicting these people at a kermis
one of these people with a peacock feather in his hair looks intently at the bagpipe player seated next to him

works.name: Netherlandish Proverbs (1559)
works.description: Dense painting illustrating 112 Dutch proverbs/idioms. Also called The Blue Cloak (woman puts blue cloak on cuckolded husband), The Topsy Turvy World, The Folly of the World. Includes: biting a column, tarts on a roof, big fish eating little fish, man belling a cat, fox and stork dining together, man defecating out window into river, man struggling to reach two loaves of bread, two men holding each other by noses, woman tying devil to a pillow, two bears dancing back to back, man biting his head against a wall. No written words (departure from Frans Hogenberg's similar print). High vantage point. Informed by Erasmus's treatise Copia per Mark Meadow.

clues.clue: Dense painting illustrating 112 Dutch proverbs including biting the column, tarts on roof, man belling a cat; also called The Blue Cloak, The Topsy Turvy World, The Folly of the World
examples: A roof is tiled with tarts in a dense, zany painting by this artist that illustrates idioms like 'biting the column'
a man in a suit of armor places a bell on a cat

clues.clue: Fox and stork dine together; fox dips head in pitcher; man defecates out window into river; man struggles to reach two loaves of bread; two men hold each other by noses; woman ties devil to a pillow; two bears dance with backs to each other
examples: a stork dips its head into a pitcher while sitting across a table from a fox
nearby a man defecates out of a window into a river
a man fails to stretch his arms to reach two loaves of bread
A woman ties the devil to a pillow in the bottom left

clues.clue: Does NOT include written words (departure from Frans Hogenberg's similar print The Blue Cloak); high vantage point similar to The Fight Between Carnival and Lent

clues.clue: Mark Meadow argues painting was informed by Erasmus's treatise Copia: Foundations of the Abundant Style
examples: Mark Meadow argues that this painting's use of thematic clusters was informed by the treatise Copia: Foundations of the Abundant Style

clues.clue: Hieronymus Cock published Big Fish Eat Little Fish drawing, initially crediting it to Bosch to popularize it
examples: Bruegel's drawing of the proverb Big Fish Eat Little Fish was published by Hieronymus Cock, who initially credited that drawing to Hieronymus Bosch

works.name: The Tower of Babel
works.description: Bruegel painted three versions; most famous now in Vienna's Kunsthistorisches Museum. May have used Colosseum engravings as basis. King Nimrod in foreground. Two men walk in wheel of giant pulley. Men haul supplies off boats near red rock slabs in lower right. Numerous crooked and dilapidated arches. Zygmunt Wazbinski argued the Vienna version contains a portrait of Alexander the Great. A smaller 1563 version shows it in orange and red with storm clouds.

clues.clue: King Nimrod stands before massive unfinished structure; may have used Colosseum engravings as basis; Vienna's Kunsthistorisches Museum version is most famous
examples: King Nimrod standing before a massive unfinished structure in one of his three versions of The Tower of Babel
Bruegel used the Roman Colosseum as a basis for his painting of this structure

clues.clue: Two men walk in wheel of giant pulley; men haul supplies off boats near red rock slabs in lower right; numerous crooked and dilapidated arches
examples: Two men walk in the wheel of a giant pulley in the most famous depiction of this subject, whose lower-right shows men hauling supplies off boats near several large red rock slabs

clues.clue: Zygmunt Wazbinski argued the Vienna version contains a portrait of Alexander the Great; 1563 'little' version shows it in orange and red with storm clouds
examples: Zygmunt Wazbinski argued that the most famous depiction of this subject contains a portrait of Alexander the Great
A 'little' painting of this subject made in 1563 shows it in orange and red with storm clouds in front of it

works.name: The Fight Between Carnival and Lent (1559)
works.description: Gaunt man holding two fish faces fat man with meat pie on head who wields a spit and straddles a beer barrel. Woman with lantern and man with bulging back led away by a fool with a torch. High vantage point (similar to Netherlandish Proverbs). Jan Miense Molenaer also painted this subject (Judith Leyster's husband).

clues.clue: Gaunt man holding two fish faces fat man with meat pie on head who wields a spit and straddles a beer barrel
examples: a gaunt man holding out a pair of fish faces off against a fat man with a meat pie on his head who wields a spit and straddles a beer barrel

clues.clue: Woman wearing a lantern and man with bulging back led away by fool with a torch

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Father of painters nicknamed 'Hell' (Pieter Brueghel the Younger) and 'Velvet' (Jan Brueghel the Elder). Other works: Mad Meg/Dull Gret (woman leading army to pillage Hell), The Triumph of Death (skeletons), The Fall of the Rebel Angels, The Blind Leading the Blind, Children's Games, The Misanthrope, The Census at Bethlehem, The Preaching of St. John the Baptist, Two Monkeys, Winter Landscape with Bird Trap, Massacre of the Innocents, The Harvesters, Big Fish Eat Little Fish (engraving). Made Large Landscapes print series with Hieronymus Cock. 'World landscape' style from Patinir.

clues.clue: Father of 'Velvet' (Jan) and 'Hell' (Pieter the Younger)

comprehensive_summary:
Pieter Bruegel the Elder (c. 1525–1569) was a Flemish Northern Renaissance painter renowned for peasant genre scenes, proverb paintings, and landscapes. He was the father of Jan 'Velvet' Brueghel and Pieter 'Hell' Brueghel the Younger, and worked with Antwerp publisher Hieronymus Cock on the Large Landscapes print series.

Landscape with the Fall of Icarus is his most commonly tested painting. A ploughman ignores Icarus's legs sticking out of the sea while a ship sails toward the setting sun; a shepherd with a brown dog leans on his staff looking skyward and a fisherman also ignores Icarus. Both extant versions (including the smaller van Buren Museum copy) are now considered early copies. The partridge, shepherd, and fisherman derive from Ovid's Metamorphoses. Andre Breton attached a cutout of the painting to 'Dusseldorf was bombed yesterday for the fiftieth time' in his First Papers of Surrealism. The painting inspired W.H. Auden's 'Musee des Beaux Arts,' William Carlos Williams's ekphrasis ('the whole pageantry of the year was awake tingling'), and Robert Baldwin argued the nearest figure represented hope per First Corinthians. It belongs to the world landscape (Weltlandschaft) tradition pioneered by Joachim Patinir.

Hunters in the Snow (1565) is the first panel of his Labors of the Months series (five of six survive: The Gloomy Day, Haymaking, The Harvesters, The Return of the Herd). Three hunters and dogs trudge through a winter landscape; one carries a dead fox; villagers skate, play ice-stock, and hockey on a frozen lake. A tavern sign on the left reads 'this is in the stag' featuring St. Hubertus and a deer. Black birds perch in trees arranged in a receding diagonal. A mill with a frozen water wheel and a woman pulled on a board toward a double-arched bridge appear in the lower right. Abbas Kiarostami used this painting for the first shot of his posthumous film 24 Frames.

Netherlandish Proverbs (1559) — also called The Blue Cloak, The Topsy Turvy World, or The Folly of the World — illustrates 112 Dutch idioms from a high vantage point. A woman puts a blue cloak on her cuckolded husband; a fox and stork dine together; a man defecates out a window; a woman ties the devil to a pillow; two bears dance with backs to each other; a man struggles to reach two loaves of bread. It does not include written words, departing from Frans Hogenberg's similar print. Mark Meadow argues it was informed by Erasmus's treatise Copia.

The Tower of Babel (three versions; most famous in Vienna's Kunsthistorisches Museum) was possibly based on Colosseum engravings. King Nimrod stands in the foreground; two men walk in the wheel of a giant pulley; men haul supplies near red rock slabs. Zygmunt Wazbinski argued the Vienna version contains a portrait of Alexander the Great.

The Fight Between Carnival and Lent (1559) shows a gaunt man with two fish facing a fat man with a meat pie on his head who straddles a beer barrel and wields a spit. The Peasant Wedding shows a feast in a barn with soup carried on a door, a boy in a red cap, and a paper-crowned bride not feasting. The Peasant Dance companion shows a devotional image of Mary on a tree ignored by the revelers.

Other major works: Mad Meg (sword-wielding woman leads army to Hell); The Triumph of Death (skeletons playing trumpets); The Fall of the Rebel Angels (Bosch-influenced transformation into fish, butterflies, gnomes); The Blind Leading the Blind (six men stumbling diagonally, which influenced Sargent's Gassed); The Harvesters (wheat field, peasants under tree); Big Fish Eat Little Fish (engraving of helmeted man attacking giant fish); Two Monkeys (chained under archway overlooking Antwerp).
